examples.title: Function Definition and Call Example
examples.description: Basic example of defining and calling a function to add two numbers
examples.explanation: The function add takes two integer parameters, computes their sum, and returns it. The prototype declares the function before main. The call passes arguments 5 and 3 which initialize parameters a and b.

examples.title: Call by Reference Using Pointers
examples.description: Demonstrates how to modify original values using pointers
examples.explanation: The swap function receives pointers to a and b. Using * operator, it accesses and modifies the original values. The addresses are passed using & operator. After call, a and b are swapped.

examples.title: Recursive Function for Factorial
examples.explanation: The function calls itself with n-1 until it reaches base case (n=0 or n=1). Each call waits for the next call to complete. Stack unwinds: factorial(5) = 5*factorial(4) = 5*4*factorial(3) = 5*4*3*factorial(2) = 5*4*3*2*factorial(1) = 5*4*3*2*1 = 120.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - due to recursive stack calls

examples.title: Function with Multiple Return Points
examples.description: Demonstrates function with early return using void return type
examples.explanation: This void function uses multiple return statements for early exit. Each return exits the function immediately without executing remaining code. The function makes decisions and prints accordingly without returning any value to the caller.
